title: Chocolate mendiants
subtitle: French chocolate rounds with dried fruit and nuts
excerpt: Small dark chocolate discs piped or spooned onto baking paper, then topped with dried fruit, nuts, and seeds before the chocolate sets. A French confectionery tradition originally associated with the four mendicant orders.
sourceNotes: Mendiants are a French confection associated with the four mendicant (begging) orders of the Catholic church: Dominicans (raisins), Augustinians (hazelnuts), Franciscans (dried figs), and Carmelites (almonds). The tradition is documented in French confectionery writing from the eighteenth century. The chocolate version replaced earlier sugar-paste forms during the nineteenth century as quality eating chocolate became accessible.

recipe.batchNotes: Scales directly. Melt chocolate in batches if scaling up significantly.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps in a cool dry place for 2 weeks. Store between layers of baking paper in an airtight tin.

content.text: Mendiants are chocolate discs topped with four things: a dried fruit, a nut, something green (pistachio, candied angelica), and sometimes a seed (sesame, poppy). The combination is traditional, but the specific toppings vary. The standard domestic method skips full tempering: the seeding trick used in chocolate bark gives a good enough snap and gloss for home use.

Arrange toppings before you start: once the chocolate discs are on the paper they set quickly and you need to move without hesitating. Lay out all the fruit, nuts, and seeds in separate small bowls within easy reach.

Line two baking trays with baking paper. Put two-thirds of the {{dark-chocolate}} in a heatproof bowl over barely simmering water. Stir until just melted. Remove from the heat and stir in the remaining chocolate until smooth and slightly thickened.

Spoon or pipe small pools of chocolate (about 1 tbsp each) onto the lined trays, spacing them well apart. Spread each pool into a round about 4-5 cm across using the back of the spoon. Immediately place one {{raisins}}, one {{blanched-hazelnuts}}, one {{shelled-pistachios}}, and one piece of {{dried-apricots}} on each disc, pressing lightly. Leave to set completely at room temperature for at least 30 minutes.
